task_completion.description: Create PANELS, ELECTRICAL, and BORDER layers with correct colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List dock in LibreCAD should display 'PANELS' with a green color swatch, 'ELECTRICAL' with a red color swatch, and 'BORDER' with a white/black color swatch.

task_completion.description: Draw four panel row rectangles on the PANELS layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main drawing area should show four identical, horizontally elongated green rectangles stacked vertically with equal spacing.

task_completion.description: Draw the combiner box rectangle on the ELECTRICAL layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main drawing area should show a small red square located to the right of the middle two green rectangles.

task_completion.description: Draw four conduit lines connecting the panels to the combiner box
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main drawing area should show four red lines starting near the right edges of the green rectangles and converging to a single point inside or near the red square.

task_completion.description: Draw the site boundary rectangle on the BORDER layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main drawing area should show a large white (or black, depending on the canvas background) rectangle that completely surrounds the panels, combiner box, and lines.
The LibreCAD window title bar or a save dialog should display 'solar_array_plan.dxf', indicating the file has been saved to the correct location.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the LibreCAD application window, not a terminal running a Python script or ezdxf commands to generate the file.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually write the DXF file in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should not show a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) being used to write DXF format text directly.

integrity.description: Agent actually executed drawing commands in LibreCAD
integrity.visual_evidence: The command line dock at the bottom of the LibreCAD window should show a history of drawing commands (e.g., 'rect', 'line') and coordinate inputs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The drawing must contain: 1. PANELS layer (Green/ACI 3) with 4 rectangles (10000x2000) at lower-left corners (0,0), (0,4000), (0,8000), (0,12000). 2. ELECTRICAL layer (Red/ACI 1) with a 600x600 rectangle at (11000, 6700) and 4 lines connecting (10000, 1000), (10000, 5000), (10000, 9000), (10000, 13000) to a hub at (11300, 7000). 3. BORDER layer (White/ACI 7) with a rectangle from (-2000, -2000) to (14000, 16000). File must be saved as ~/Documents/LibreCAD/solar_array_plan.dxf.
